primary_method.name: DDM
primary_method.reasoning: Banks should not be valued with DCF since FCF is not meaningful. DDM + Excess Returns + Justified P/B is the standard banking framework.

inputs.rf_reasoning: 5.75% BI-Rate current. Used as risk-free for transparency: BFIN's funding cost is directly BI-Rate dependent.
inputs.erp_reasoning: 8.0%: Damodaran Indonesia 7.5% + 50bp for non-bank financial premium and current macro stress (rupiah record low, aggressive hike cycle).
inputs.beta_reasoning: yfinance equity beta vs local index.
inputs.d0_reasoning: Sustainable dividend per share. user override.
inputs.book_value_reasoning: Total equity IDR 10,652.929B (FY2025 balance sheet, databundle) divided by 14.749B shares = IDR 722.3 per share.
inputs.roe_reasoning: ROE 14.8% per databundle FY2025 (NI 1,582B / equity 10,653B = 14.85%). Current trajectory lower (Q1 2026 annualized ~13.3%). Base normalized to 16.5% by FY2028.
inputs.g_high_reasoning: 7.5% NI CAGR Stage 1: argued from receivables growth (6.5%), partial GP margin recovery, operating leverage. See roe_normalized reasoning above.
inputs.g_terminal_reasoning: 5.5%: 2.0% real + 3.5% Indonesian inflation. Below nominal GDP to account for penetration limits in the multifinance sector.
inputs.g_book_reasoning: 5.28% = ROE(16.5%) x retention(32%). The endogenous book growth from retained earnings at the base case NI and payout.

excess_returns.formula: Excess Returns value = BV_0 + sum( (ROE - Ke) x BV_t / (1+Ke)^t )

justified_pb.formula: Justified P/B = (ROE - g) / (Ke - g);  Implied value = Justified P/B x Book value

blended.formula: Blended value = (0.40 x DDM) + (0.40 x ER) + (0.20 x Justified P/B)

cross_check.name: Peer P/B comparison
cross_check.reasoning: ADMF (Adira Finance, bank-backed) trades at 0.64x P/B with ROE 12.3%. CFIN (Clipan Finance) at 0.25x P/B with ROE 4%. BBRI (BRI bank) at 1.32x P/B with ROE 18.1%. BFIN at 0.93x P/B with ROE 14.8% is premium to the two direct multifinance peers but below a bank with similar ROE. The 0.93x P/B is arguably CHEAP vs BBRI (1.32x, ROE 18%) if BFIN's ROE normalizes above 15%. Multifinance peers trade at extreme discounts because ADMF's NI fell 27% in 2024 (far worse than BFIN's 1% decline) and CFIN is subscale. BFIN's superior credit quality (NPF 1.25% vs sector 2.70%) and lower leverage (D/E 1.28x vs sector) justify a premium over ADMF. A fundamental fair P/B for BFIN above ADMF's 0.64x and below BBRI's 1.32x is 0.9-1.5x, with our base at 2.18x justified P/B only achievable at full ROE normalization.

peer_data.note: Direct peer; subscale, unprofitable by quality
Anchor bank; higher quality but deposit-funded (lower cost of funds)

inputs.note: At our target 1.20x forward P/B, BFIN trades between ADMF (0.64x) and BBRI (1.32x) -- appropriate given its ROE is between ADMF and BBRI.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.probability_reasoning: 30% probability: BI rate hike cycle on June 18 2026 (today) is the trigger signal. Mechanism is clear and proximate: rupiah at record weak (USD/IDR ~18,000), BI in active tightening cycle. This bear requires TWO simultaneous sustained headwinds -- persistent rate elevation AND competitive share loss. The second (competitive share loss) is not yet confirmed by BFIN data (BFIN grew receivables 9.6% in 9M 2024 despite Mandiri Tunas +15.8%). Theia grades this bear as MECHANISM (not OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR). 30% reflects: it's real, proximate, and the second mechanism is building, but not yet fully triggered.
scenarios.reasoning: Bear: BI-Rate re-tightens to 6.0%+ (100bp in 30 days already done), keeping funding costs elevated. Ke rises to 12% in this scenario as the market continues to price crisis risk. Car sales decline continues (5-7% p.a.). Bank-backed competition (Mandiri Tunas) takes incremental share. GP margin does not recover (stays 63-64%). Provision re-accelerates toward 3.5-4% of receivables. NI falls from 1,582B to 1,360B by FY2028. ROE at ~12% -- below Ke, meaning the company destroys value in this scenario. Evidence quality per Theia: MECHANISM (causal pathway clear, BI re-tightening already occurring). Note: even in this bear, the 10%+ dividend yield (70 IDR on 690 IDR price) provides cash income that partially offsets capital loss.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.probability_reasoning: 50% probability: the honest most-likely given observable evidence (H1 2025 NI +11.1%, NPF 1.25% -- best in sector, car sales recovering 12.4% YTD 2026, dividend yield 10% providing floor). The base requires BI-Rate to stabilize (not necessarily cut -- just halt the hike cycle) and provision to continue normalizing. The base does NOT require BI rate cuts in 2026 -- only stabilization at current 5.75% with gradual easing in 2027. Given that Indonesia's inflation is contained (within BI target) and the rate hikes are rupiah-defence driven, there is a credible path to hike cessation in H2 2026.
scenarios.reasoning: Base: BI-Rate stabilizes at 5.75% in H2 2026, begins easing in 2027 toward 5.0%. Receivables grow 6.5% p.a. (used-car recovery, multipurpose/sharia growth). CKPN normalizes to 2.5% of receivables (from ~3.5% peak). GP margin recovers 30-50bp as cost of funds falls. OpEx grows 5% (below wage inflation but operating leverage positive at 6.5% revenue growth). NI grows from 1,582B to 1,830B by FY2028. ROE recovers from 14.8% (FY2025) to 16.5% (FY2028). Note: I apply a 100bp haircut to Theia's 17.5% ROE target given the June 18 rate hike delays the easing timeline.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.probability_reasoning: 20% probability: requires both deep BI easing (to 4.5% by end-2027) and strong multipurpose/sharia growth (11% receivables CAGR). The sharia growth (39.2% yoy in 2024) is the most credible bull catalyst -- BFIN has a first-mover advantage in BPKB-secured sharia financing for the Indonesian Muslim majority. Heavy equipment is a secondary bull catalyst (Prabowo infrastructure program). Rate easing is EXTRAPOLATED per Theia. The bull requires no renewed credit cycle deterioration. 20% reflects: high-growth scenario elements are real but the amplitude requires favorable macro AND company-specific execution.
scenarios.reasoning: Bull: BI-Rate eases to 4.5% by end-2027 (global rate decline + IDR stabilization + contained inflation). Cost of funds falls 150bp from peak, restoring 120-150bp of GP margin. BFIN sharia financing grows 30%+ per annum (from 39.2% base in 2024). Heavy equipment benefits from Prabowo infrastructure acceleration. Provision normalizes fully to 1.8-2.0% of receivables. NI reaches 2,480B by FY2028 (ROE 21%). This is the scenario where BFIN demonstrates it can sustain above-cycle ROE from the diversification of its business mix away from car-dependent yields. Evidence quality per Theia: EXTRAPOLATED. Note: in this scenario, the P/B would re-rate toward 3.07x (2,217 IDR/share) -- implying 222% upside over 3 years from today's 690 IDR.

sensitivity_matrix.metric: implied P/B x BV = IDR per share
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: Ke is the dominant lever in the justified P/B framework. A 100bp change in Ke moves the value by 200-400 IDR/share at current ROE levels. ROE is second-order: a 100bp change in ROE moves the value by ~150-200 IDR/share. The call is primarily a bet on Ke normalization from crisis 14% to fundamental 10.5%.
sensitivity_matrix.note: Current price 690 IDR = P/B 0.955x. At Ke=10.5%, ROE=14%: 1,228 IDR (77% upside). At Ke=11.5%, ROE=14%: 1,023 IDR (48% upside). All cells above current price -- asymmetric risk/reward.
